content: Summarize the deployment pipeline for the warehouse robotics system

The deployment pipeline uses ArgoCD with a lint → test → integration → canary (5% for 30min) → full rollout flow. Deploys happen every Tuesday and Thursday. Rollbacks are automatic if error rate exceeds 0.1%. Emergency hotfixes can bypass canary with VP approval and require a 48-hour post-incident review.
What are the current performance bottlenecks?

Three main bottlenecks: (1) Mean pick time is 4.2s vs 3.5s target — arm trajectory planning needs optimization. (2) Robot uptime is 98.1% vs 99.5% target — firmware v3.2 with improved LiDAR processing should help. (3) MTTR is 12min vs 10min target — the SRE team is working on faster incident detection.

output: Generated quarterly report for warehouse ops

message: New alert from #ops-incidents: Robot R-142 offline at Newark facility

content: Robot R-142 is offline at Newark. What's the escalation path and who's on call?

Escalation path: on-call engineer → team lead → VP Engineering → CTO. On-call rotation is weekly and shared across all teams. For a single robot offline, the on-call SRE should investigate first. If it's a fleet-wide issue, escalate to the platform team lead immediately.
